title: For Event Organisers
subtitle: Deaf access consultancy and sign language interpreting that fits your event - not the other way around.
metaDescription: Performance Interpreting provides BSL and ISL interpreting for festivals, sport, arenas and live entertainment across the UK and Ireland. Expert logistics, NRCPD-registered interpreters, end-to-end coordination.

sections.label: What We Offer
sections.heading: Deaf access consultancy and full-service interpreting
body: We don't just supply interpreters - we consult, plan and deliver. From advising on your legal obligations under the Equality Act 2010 (England, Scotland, Wales), the Disability Discrimination Act 1995 (Northern Ireland) and the Irish Sign Language Act 2017 (Ireland), through to designing your access plan, coordinating with your production team and managing the interpreter team on the day. That end-to-end approach is what separates PI from agencies that just send a name and a DBS check.
Every interpreter we place is NRCPD-registered. For large events, we deploy interpreter teams with rotations built in - so quality and accuracy never dips over a long day.

items.title: BSL & ISL Interpreting
items.description: British Sign Language interpreting for UK events. Suitable for concerts, festivals, sport, theatre, comedy, awards and more.

items.title: ISL Interpreting
items.description: Irish Sign Language interpreting for events in Ireland and Northern Ireland.

items.title: Deaf Access Packages
items.description: Full access coordination including BSL, ISL, live captions and induction loop systems - tailored to your venue and audience.

items.title: On-site Coordination
items.description: A PI coordinator attends large events to manage the interpreter team, liaise with production and adapt in real time.

sections.label: How It Works
sections.heading: From enquiry to curtain up
body: We've refined the process over hundreds of events. We remove the barriers for your team while ensuring nothing is missed on the day.

items.title: 1. Tell us about your event
items.description: Share the basics - event type, venue, date, expected audience size. We'll come back with questions and a plan outline.

items.title: 2. We build your access plan
items.description: We confirm interpreter numbers, team composition, rotation schedules and venue logistics. You get a clear plan you can share with your production team.

items.title: 3. Pre-event briefing
items.description: We brief interpreters on your programme - setlist, speakers, running order, any specialist vocabulary. Thorough prep is what separates good interpreting from excellent interpreting.

items.title: 4. On-the-day delivery
items.description: Our team arrives early, coordinates with your crew on placement and lighting and delivers throughout the event. We manage the unexpected so you don't have to.

sections.label: Why PI
sections.heading: The difference between a supplier and a consultancy
body: Most agencies will provide a name and a DBS check. They'll let you figure out positioning, lighting, rotation schedules and legal compliance on your own. We've seen the results of that approach - interpreters placed where the audience can't see them, no rotation plan for a twelve-hour festival and organisers exposed to legal risk they didn't know existed.

Performance Interpreting operates as a consultancy. We advise on your obligations, design your access plan, brief interpreters on your specific event, coordinate with your production team and manage everything on the day. Our interpreters are specialists in live events - they know how to read a room, adapt to a live setlist change and work within production constraints. That's why Wembley, The O2, Live Nation and the Premier League choose PI - a service that integrates seamlessly with theirs.

items.title: Event-specialist interpreters
items.description: Our team has specific experience in live event settings - including high-energy music, stadium sport and large-scale theatre.

items.title: NRCPD-registered, every time
items.description: No exceptions. Every interpreter holds current NRCPD registration - the standard required by public sector bodies and courts.

items.title: Proven at scale
items.description: 600+ events including six Premier League clubs, national arenas and the UK's biggest festival circuit.

items.title: Legal compliance confidence
items.description: Following our testimony in the Little Mix BSL case, we understand the legal landscape inside out. We can advise on your access obligations as well as deliver them.
